sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Oil States selects CFO Hadjik as new President and CEO with Taylor set to retire (OIS:NYSE)
feed.summary: Oil States International announced that its current President and CEO, Cindy Taylor, will retire, effective May 1. Lloyd Hajdik, the company's Executive VP and CFO, has been appointed as the new President and CEO. This leadership change marks a significant transition for the company.

feed.title: BofA Securities reinstates Oracle stock coverage with buy rating
feed.summary: BofA Securities has reinstated coverage on Oracle Corp. (NYSE:ORCL) with a buy rating and a price target of $200, indicating a potential 30% upside. This decision is driven by expected accelerating AI infrastructure demand and Oracle's substantial $553 billion in remaining performance obligations, despite the capital-intensive nature of its ongoing transformation. The firm anticipates meaningful growth, seeing the stock as undervalued with 17 analysts revising earnings upwards for the upcoming period.

feed.title: EQT Announces Early Results and Upsizing of its Tender Offer for Certain Senior Notes
feed.summary: EQT Corporation announced the early results and upsizing of its tender offer to purchase for cash certain senior notes. The company increased the maximum aggregate purchase price for all notes from $1.15 billion to $1.4 billion, and increased the sub-cap for specific 2029 notes to $1.0 billion. Due to the high volume of early tenders exceeding the aggregate offer cap, EQT does not expect to accept any further tenders after the early tender date.

feed.title: Prudential Advisors’ Moira Buckley tapped for Finseca leadership track toward presidency
feed.summary: Moira Buckley, a senior leader at Prudential Advisors, has been appointed secretary of Finseca, setting her on a four-year path to the organization's presidency by 2028-29. With over three decades of experience at Prudential, Buckley's selection highlights her commitment to expanding access to comprehensive financial advice and strengthening the profession, particularly by promoting women in leadership. Finseca aims to advance financial security for Americans through advocacy and elevating professional standards, a mission Buckley is well-positioned to further.

feed.title: Murphy Middleton Hinkle & Parker Inc. Boosts Stake in Bank of New York Mellon
feed.summary: Investment management firm Murphy Middleton Hinkle & Parker Inc. significantly increased its stake in The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation by 5,829.6% during the fourth quarter of 2025. The firm now holds 142,191 shares worth approximately $16.5 million, making BNY Mellon its 5th largest portfolio position and accounting for 4.7% of its total assets under management. This move suggests the firm sees significant long-term value and growth potential in BNY Mellon's financial services business.

feed.title: The Quick and Easy Way to See if You Can Get the Amex Platinum (and a Big Welcome Bonus Offer)
feed.summary: The American Express Platinum Card is offering a potentially huge welcome bonus, and applicants can check their eligibility and exact offer amount without impacting their credit score. The article details how to apply and see the offer, highlighting benefits like up to 175,000 Membership Rewards Points and over $3,500 in annual perks. It also discusses credit score considerations and Amex's "once-in-a-lifetime rule" for welcome bonuses.

feed.title: Outlook Therapeutics Announces Pricing of $5.0 Million Public Offering
feed.summary: Outlook Therapeutics, Inc. announced the pricing of a public offering totaling $5.0 million. The offering includes 20,000,000 shares of common stock and accompanying warrants, each sold at a combined price of $0.25. The net proceeds are intended for working capital and general corporate purposes, with the closing expected around March 25, 2026.

feed.title: 20M new shares at 25 cents: Outlook prices $5M fundraise
feed.summary: Outlook Therapeutics (OTLK) has priced a public offering of 20,000,000 shares and accompanying warrants at a combined price of $0.25 per share/warrant, aiming to raise $5.0 million in gross proceeds. The offering is expected to close around March 25, 2026, with proceeds intended for working capital and general corporate purposes. Following this announcement, OTLK shares experienced a significant decline of 24.77%, highlighting market concerns about dilution.

feed.title: Who's Moving Where In Wealth Management? – Broadridge, Baird
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions has appointed Peter Reali as senior vice president and general manager of institutional governance, a new role focused on advancing proxy voting and stewardship solutions. Baird, an employee-owned wealth management firm, has hired Bryan Vaughn as a director and financial advisor in its Fort Worth, Texas office. Vaughn, who joins from Ameriprise Financial Services, brings two decades of industry experience and managed over $160 million in assets previously.

feed.title: Why Is OTLK Stock Falling Today?
feed.summary: Outlook Therapeutics (OTLK) shares plummeted 27% in pre-market trading after the company announced a proposed public offering of its common stock and accompanying warrants. This offering comes after the FDA issued a Complete Response Letter in December 2025 for their wet AMD treatment, ONS-5010 / LYTENAVA, and recent debt financing. Retail sentiment on Stocktwits is "extremely bearish" due to the undisclosed size of the offering and lack of clear plans for the proceeds, with the stock already down significantly year-to-date.

This dual recognition highlights Logility's commitment to innovation and delivering measurable outcomes for customers in diverse industrial sectors.

feed.title: Chevron CEO Warns Oil Markets Still Aren't Pricing In Full Supply Shock From Iran's Strait Of Hormuz Closure Despite 60% Price Surge
feed.summary: Chevron CEO Mike Wirth stated that oil and gas futures markets are not fully reflecting the significant supply disruptions caused by the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, despite a 60% increase in oil prices. He emphasized that the situation is very tight, with close to 20% of global oil and LNG flows affected, alongside critical supplies for agriculture and semiconductors. Wirth added that Asia is already experiencing severe shortages, and even if the strait reopens, a full recovery will take time, with current emergency reserve releases falling short of making up for the lost supply.

feed.title: Cardinal Health sets April 30 webcast for fiscal Q3 results

Historically, such earnings date announcements have led to mild positive stock reactions.

feed.title: Nio's battery asset operator Mirattery lists $72.7 million ABS in Shanghai
feed.summary: Mirattery, Nio's battery asset operator, has listed a 501-million-yuan ($72.7 million) asset-backed security (ABS) on the Shanghai Stock Exchange. This marks a significant move in asset securitization, aiming to enhance liquidity, attract investors, and improve capital efficiency for the company's battery-as-a-service (BaaS) operations. The listing is the world's first holding-type power battery ABS and follows other recent financing efforts by Mirattery.

feed.title: Inotiv receives waiver of minimum liquidity covenant for March test dates
feed.summary: Inotiv, Inc. (NASDAQ: NOTV) announced that its lenders have granted a waiver of the minimum liquidity covenant under its Credit Agreement for the March 20 and March 27, 2026 liquidity test dates. This waiver is limited to the specific test dates and does not amend other provisions of the agreement. The news follows Inotiv's Q1 2026 earnings missing market expectations and a reported cybersecurity breach.

feed.title: Nektar Therapeutics Faces High-Stakes AAD Data Test as Sky-High Valuation Leaves Little Room for Error
feed.summary: Nektar Therapeutics' stock has surged 471% over the past year, leading to an extremely high valuation with a Price-to-Sales ratio of 38.6x, driven by optimism for its drug candidate, rezpegaldesleukin, for autoimmune markets. While recent Phase 2b data for atopic dermatitis and alopecia areata was positive and supports Phase 3 advancement, the market has already priced in a blockbuster outcome, leaving little room for error. The upcoming AAD data presentation is a critical catalyst, as the data needs to demonstrate a compelling advantage over existing therapies to justify the current premium valuation and avoid a sharp re-rating.
